impacts.statement: Major flooding, flow in Plum Creek is seventy five to one hundred yards wide in most areas with many secondary roads and low bridges flooded. Livestock in low areas of the flood plain may be cut off and drowned.
Moderate lowland flooding reaches significant areas of the flood plain flooding secondary roads and low bridges. Livestock in the flood plain may be cut off in low areas and drowned.
Minor lowland flooding escapes banks into lower sections of the flood plain. Briggs Road near the bridge begins to flood.
Flood waters encroaches on the FM 1322 bridge approach.
Flow spills over low sections of banks.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
